subject: Travelers VA-Carolina + Mid-Atlantic Claim Center Adjuster Roster — captured from 4 threads
denialText: Travelers Virginia-Carolina Claim Center is staffed with a known roster. Template letters use the same phrasing across adjusters: '24/7 access to your claim online via MyTravelers' + 'PO Box 430, Buffalo NY 14240-0430' + manager-escalation pattern + per-adjuster phone routing. The patent-disclosed automation (Travelers operates 700+ FAA-certified drone pilots and is one of the top 3 P&C AI patent holders per Insurance Journal 2025) handles initial scoping; adjusters confirm + adjust per script.

adjusterRoster.name: Peter Fouche
adjusterRoster.title: Claim Professional - Claim Response Team
adjusterRoster.exampleClaim: JEE9334 (The Automobile Insurance Company of Hartford CT)

templatePhrases: Everything You Need at Your Fingertips
With 24/7 access to your claim online via MyTravelers®; you can check your claim status, view your deductible, interact with us and upload documents.
If further assistance is required, please contact my manager [...]
Available to Insureds and Contractors - To upload documents to your claim, please use this link: Claim Upload Center
This message (including any attachments) may contain confidential, proprietary, privileged and/or private information
Per the Loss Settlement provision of the policy, we pay no more than the actual cash value (ACV) until the actual repair or replacement is complete
The initial payment is based on the age of the materials
keyDenialLanguage: the hail consideration to the shingles was determined to not be from hail
instead was a result of granule loss and blistering
I am standing by my decision that he is pointing out normal wear and tear to the shingles rather than damage due to hail
Your estimate includes depreciation which is based on the age and condition of the damaged items
Hover report showing where our measurements come from
carrierTactic: Travelers VA-Carolina Claim Center uses a standardized template across ~11 known adjusters. Wear-and-tear classification is the default for granule loss/blistering. ACV-first payment structure with depreciation held until completion. Hover.com aerial measurements as scope basis. Drone-based inspections (Travelers operates 700+ FAA-certified drone pilots).
rooferTactic: Know which manager to escalate to per adjuster (each has a documented manager email in their sig block). Use Chris Aycock's manufacturer-spec counter (see TravCo JEE8887 dump) for slope-coverage disputes — bypass damage classification, argue manufacturer installation requirements. For depreciation/ACV disputes, request the depreciation schedule basis.
patentMapping: Travelers is one of the top 3 P&C AI patent holders per Insurance Journal 2025 (238 patents filed Sept 2024, 72 issued in last 5 years). Their AI determination of roof condition from aerial imagery + 700+ FAA-certified drone pilots = patent-driven scoping. Per-adjuster phone routing keeps the same template language stable across the team — strong boilerplate-match opportunity.
lessonForAnalyzer: Travelers denial pattern: (1) Identify which Claim Center (VA-Carolina vs Mid-Atlantic vs other) — manager hierarchy differs. (2) Same template language across adjusters = strong boilerplate match signal even on new adjuster names. (3) For coverage scope disputes, prefer manufacturer-spec arguments over damage-classification challenges. (4) For payment disputes, ACV-first + depreciation withholding is standard — request line-item Xactimate + depreciation methodology basis.
